claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

- Is your child **under 16 years old**?
- Is your child **between 16 and 19 years old**?
- Is your child **20 years old or over**?

Please let me know your child's age (or which category they fall into), and if you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I've got two children I'd like to check on - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 20.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Based on what you've told me:

- **Alex (age 8)**: Since Alex is under 16, they automatically qualify for the age requirement. Let me continue checking the other eligibility criteria for Alex.
- **Blake (age 20)**: Since Blake is 20 or over, I need to check this path as well.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Here's where we stand:

---

**For Blake (age 20):**
Unfortunately, Blake does not meet the age eligibility criteria. Child Benefit is only available for children who are:
- Under 16 years old, OR
- Aged 16-19 and in approved education or training

Since Blake is 20 years old, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake**.

---

**For Alex (age 8):**
Alex meets the age requirement. Now I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

*(At age 8, I'd expect Alex isn't working, but I need to confirm this to proceed through the official assessment.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, Alex is only 8, so no, they're not working at all - they're in primary school. And yes, Blake is 20, so I understand about that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes complete sense - Alex is in primary school and not working, so that's a "yes" to that question. Let me continue the assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, let's continue with Alex's assessment.

**Next question: Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that a young person might claim themselves. 

At age 8, I would expect Alex isn't receiving any such benefits - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't get any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Let me continue with the next check for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

At age 8 and in primary school, I'd expect not - but just to confirm: Alex is not in any kind of apprenticeship, correct?

*(For reference: Most apprenticeships would disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except for Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're just 8 and in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Let me continue with the assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits like Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient financial resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You are a **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad. None of those other situations apply to me either.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Let me check the outcome for your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I now have enough information to provide you with the eligibility outcome for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Outcome

I'm sorry to say that based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for either of your children**. Here's the breakdown:

---

### **Alex (age 8)** ❌ Not Eligible

Alex meets all the child-related criteria:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ Not in an apprenticeship

**However**, you do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or qualify through an alternative path (such as settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant). Since you're living abroad and none of these apply, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

---

### **Blake (age 20)** ❌ Not Eligible

Blake exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit. The benefit is only available for children who are:
- Under 16, or
- Aged 16-19 and in approved education or training

At age 20, Blake no longer qualifies regardless of other circumstances.

---

### **What You Can Do**

- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you move back to the UK or become a Crown servant), you may become eligible to claim for Alex.
- For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex (age 8) meets the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant does not normally live in the UK and does not qualify through any of the alternative paths (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or Crown servant status). Therefore, even though Alex would otherwise be a qualifying child, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex due to not meeting residency requirements.

Blake is 20 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is only available for children who are under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. At age 20, Blake does not qualify as an eligible child regardless of other circumstances.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. Blake (age 20) does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit. Alex (age 8) would meet all the child-related criteria, but the claimant does not satisfy the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and do not qualify through any alternative residency paths (such as settled/pre-settled status or Crown servant status).

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
